film.title: Rang
film.overview: Yogi Joshi (Kamal Sadanah) is the hardworking middle class student who goes to college with the traditional Pooja (Ayesha Julka), who is the daughter of a single-father. Pooja has been in love with Yogi for quite some time, but Yogi seems to see her as a good friend.  Yogi's father (Bharat Kapoor) works at the factory owned by the successful Indu Singh (Amrita Singh). Indu admits her daughter into the same college as Yogi and Pooja. She is a trustee of the college and is assured by the Principal that Kajal is in good hands. Kajal (Divya Bharti) goes to college and tries to park in Pooja's spot, but Pooja cuts her off. The two get in an altercation and to show Pooja how much power Kajal has, she drives her jeep into the corridors of the college and stops right in front of Pooja and her friends, including Yogi.

dialogues.dialogue: Ma'am Indu, please make way for our new chairperson. Are we in such dire straits, that a man....who was a mere worker in this company a few years ago....is being made the chairperson today? Has your standard fallen so much? How shameful! It's not shameful, but a matter of pride, Ms. Suchitra. My becoming the chairperson proves....that if an ordinary worker....works hard he can become the chairman of this company.

Are we in such dire straits, that a man....who was a mere worker in this company a few years ago....is being made the chairperson today? Has your standard fallen so much? How shameful! It's not shameful, but a matter of pride, Ms. Suchitra. My becoming the chairperson proves....that if an ordinary worker....works hard he can become the chairman of this company. It's the company's magnanimity....the credit for which goes to you all.
Only to defeat you. You consider love to be a game? A play? You should feel ashamed! Truly speaking, I'm ashamed. That's why I've come to apologise.
You consider love to be a game? A play? You should feel ashamed! Truly speaking, I'm ashamed. That's why I've come to apologise. There are some mistakes that can't be pardoned.

Are we in such dire straits, that a man....who was a mere worker in this company a few years ago....is being made the chairperson today? Has your standard fallen so much? How shameful! It's not shameful, but a matter of pride, Ms. Suchitra. My becoming the chairperson proves....that if an ordinary worker....works hard he can become the chairman of this company. It's the company's magnanimity....the credit for which goes to you all.
She's a very nice girl. I know her. Crazy, proud and reared by a selfish mom. The only child of a rich family. The darling of Mrs. Indu. Laxmi, they're super rich people.
dialogues.cause_raw: being the only child of a rich family
